---
title: "Prototypes & Design Evolution"
subtitle: "Wireframes, design decisions, and iteration history"
---

## Overview

This page documents the design process behind the final dashboards — from initial per-task sketches through to the refined 9-dashboard finding-based layout. Each iteration addressed specific clarity or coverage issues identified during design review.

---

## Design Principles

The dashboards follow five core principles:

1. **One conclusion per view** — each dashboard communicates exactly one finding
2. **Dataset toggle** — the shared `Dataset_Label` filter lets users switch between FILAH, TROUT, and Journalist across all charts simultaneously
3. **Consistent colour palette** — fishing = blue, tourism = purple, neutral = green — never deviated
4. **No repeated charts** — every chart appears on exactly one dashboard
5. **Fixed 1200 × 800px** — tiled layout, no floating objects

---

## Dashboard Layout Plan

The final website maps to 9 Tableau dashboards:

| Dashboard | Finding | Key Questions |
|---|---|---|
| Dashboard 0 | Overview / Entry Point | Orientation |
| Dashboard 1 | Finding 1 | What each lobby left out |
| Dashboard 1.1 | Finding 1 (continued) | How exclusions distort the rankings |
| Dashboard 2 | Finding 2 | Full committee balance |
| Dashboard 2.1 | Bridge | How selective reporting creates a false narrative |
| Dashboard 3 | Finding 3 | Sentiment patterns over time |
| Dashboard 3.1 | Finding 3 (continued) | Member travel behaviour |
| Dashboard 4 | Finding 4 | Member overview and influence |
| Dashboard 4.1 | Finding 4 (continued) | The omission case — FILAH, TROUT, and Tante Titan |

---

## Version 1 — Per-Task Sketches

The first version was a hand-drawn sketch for each MC2 task question. One sketch per task, designed independently before any dashboard structure was decided.

<img src="./images/Initial Wireframe.jpg" style="width: 100%; height: auto;" alt="Tableau Schedule">

## Version 2 — Finding-Based Layout (Final)

After reviewing the per-task sketches, charts serving the same narrative arc were grouped into shared dashboards. Tasks were not kept as individual dashboards — instead, the structure was reorganised around four findings plus an overview entry point.

Key decisions made at this stage:

- Tasks 4a and 4b were co-located because the member omission evidence and the Tante Titan profile tell one continuous story
- Dashboard 2.1 was added as a bridge to make the mechanism of bias manipulation explicit before moving to the full-record findings
- Dashboard 3 was split into D3 (sentiment) and D3.1 (travel) to avoid chart density
- Dashboard 4 was split into D4 (member overview) and D4.1 (omission case) for the same reason

---

## Colour System

The colour palette was fixed at the start of design to avoid visual inconsistency.

::: {.key-numbers}
**Fishing:** blue
**Tourism:** purple
**Neutral:** green
:::

---

## Chart Type Selection

Each chart type was chosen to support a specific analytical task:

| Chart Type | Used For | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| 100% stacked bar | Industry share per dataset | Shows proportional split; comparison across 3 groups |
| Diverging bar (lollipop) | Bias scores per member | Centred on zero; direction and magnitude visible |
| Slope graph | Participation rank shifts | Highlights rank reversals caused by selective inclusion |
| Scatter/quadrant | Fishing vs tourism participation | Shows cluster structure; identifies balanced vs polarised members |
| Heatmap (member × topic) | Sentiment patterns | Compact grid; colour encodes sentiment direction |
| Treemap | Proportional coverage | Size = volume; nesting = member → industry hierarchy |
| Bubble chart | Influence × bias | Two-dimension encoding: bias score and total activity |
| Stacked area + line | Sentiment over time | Longitudinal trend; meeting gap visible as axis break |
| Geographic map | Member travel | Spatial distribution of travel activity |
| Attendance heatmap | Meeting presence | Matrix view; absences visible as blank cells |

---

## Iteration Log

| Version | Change | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| v1 | Per-task sketches (8 sketches, one per question) | Initial design exploration before dashboard structure was decided |
| v1 → v2 | Regrouped into finding-based dashboards | Task-based splits separated charts that tell one continuous story |
| v2 | Added Dashboard 2.1 bridge | Makes the bias mechanism explicit before full-record analysis |
| v2 | Split Dashboard 3 → D3 + D3.1 | Four charts in 1200×800px was too dense; sentiment and travel are distinct findings |
| v2 | Split Dashboard 4 → D4 + D4.1 | Six charts in 1200×800px was too dense; member overview and omission case are distinct |
| Final | Added Person-Picker dashboard action in D4 | Interactive filter lets evaluators explore all 6 members without adding new charts |
